scenario: Raix work session - extend the tool-calling pipeline

map_quality.rationale: The answer hands the agent a complete map: chat loop (chat_completion.rb), function DSL (function_dispatch.rb), schema/adapters (function_tool_adapter.rb, message_adapters/base.rb, transcript_adapter.rb, multimodal_content_adapter.rb), and MCP integration (mcp.rb, mcp/tool.rb, mcp/sse_client.rb, mcp/stdio_client.rb). The agent can pick any entry point with only small targeted lookups; only minor gap is not explicitly calling out the mixin composition pattern in the map itself.

specificity.rationale: Every component is named with a concrete path (e.g., `lib/raix/chat_completion.rb`, `lib/raix/function_dispatch.rb`, `lib/raix/mcp/sse_client.rb`) and tied to the module/responsibility — no vague 'the chat code' references.

justification.rationale: Each file gets a short purpose blurb (e.g., `function_tool_adapter.rb` 'converts Raix function declarations into RubyLLM::Tool subclass instances'), but the answer never explicitly explains WHY the design is a set of composable mixins included into a host class — it describes pieces without framing the composition rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer makes no acknowledgment of uncertainty about how the mixins compose into a host class or any other assumption — it reads as fully confident throughout.

steps.step: Trace a tool-call round-trip through the chat loop

map_quality.rationale: Provides a complete, ordered trace with file:line for every layer: entry at chat_completion.rb:73, tool detection at line 160, dispatch at 186, transcript append in function_dispatch.rb:67-106, and recursion at 195-209. An agent could follow this path to modify tool-call handling with minimal lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every step names the exact method and file:line (chat_completion.rb:340 for ruby_llm_request, :383-384 for tool conversion, :160 for tool_calls detection, :186 for dispatch, :298 for public_send). No vague references.

justification.rationale: Explains why the loop continues (recursion with messages: nil re-reads transcript, decrementing max_tool_calls) and how results re-enter via the two-element transcript append. Could go slightly deeper on why messages: nil triggers re-read, but the mechanism is clear.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the three termination branches (plain response, max_tool_calls exceeded, stop_tool_calls_and_respond!) at specific lines, which covers the key branches an agent must verify. Doesn't explicitly call out uncertainty, but enumerates the branch points clearly.

steps.step: Map how functions are declared and exposed to the model

map_quality.rationale: Covers the full path with precise file:line: declaration (`function_dispatch.rb:46-107`), storage (`@functions` line 27), tools shape (`tools` 126-130), filtering (`filtered_tools` 303-313), and schema conversion via `FunctionToolAdapter` plus RubyLLM registration. An agent could add a new declared capability with only small lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is named with file and line ranges (e.g. `lib/raix/function_dispatch.rb:46-107`, `function_tool_adapter.rb:50-57`, `chat_completion.rb:382-384`), not vague references — exactly the granularity the agent needs to edit.

justification.rationale: Explains WHAT each step does and notes it produces 'the OpenAI tools schema shape', but doesn't quite step back to articulate WHY this is a class-level DSL converted to schema (declarative host-class capability) — the reasoning is implicit.

uncertainty.rationale: No flagging of error-prone areas (parameter JSON-schema shape, name collisions, filtering semantics, RubyLLM adapter quirks). The answer reads as fully confident with no callouts for the agent to double-check.

steps.step: Map how the conversation is adapted before it is sent

map_quality.rationale: Covers all three adapter layers (TranscriptAdapter, MessageAdapters::Base, MultimodalContentAdapter) with file:line and ties each into the chat flow (chat_completion.rb:139, :358). Bonus before-completion hooks layer extends the map; agent could act with only small follow-ups.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file paths and line numbers throughout (`message_adapters/base.rb:17-27`, `multimodal_content_adapter.rb:20`, `chat_completion.rb:139`, `:358`). Each transformation point is precisely located rather than abstract.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY for each: TranscriptAdapter normalizes abbreviated vs full formats, Base handles legacy/cache-shape, MultimodalContentAdapter detects image arrays and decodes base64. Could be slightly more explicit about provider-shape normalization as a unifying motive but the per-piece reasoning is there.

uncertainty.rationale: Implicitly flags conditional branches (Claude-3 cache_at, image_url detection, legacy role conversion) but doesn't explicitly call out uncertainty or where the agent should verify. The structure surfaces conditionality without naming risk.

steps.step: Map how external tools plug into the same dispatch

map_quality.rationale: Maps Tool value object (mcp/tool.rb:11-66), both transport clients (sse_client.rb, stdio_client.rb), and the registration path in mcp.rb:70-162 showing how MCP tools register via FunctionDispatch.function and define instance methods that dispatch_tool_function can call. The agent has a complete picture of how external and local tools share one mechanism.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout — `lib/raix/mcp/tool.rb:11-66`, `lib/raix/mcp.rb:70-162`, line 96, line 108, line 110, line 125-126, line 168-189, plus cross-references to chat_completion.rb:184 and 195-209. No vague phrasing.

justification.rationale: The 'Joining dispatch' section explicitly explains WHY they share one path: registered in @functions so tools/filtered_tools include them, instance methods callable via public_send, same transcript format so continuation logic works identically — not just a file list.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the schema-source difference (input_schema overwrites latest_definition[:parameters] at line 110) and names two distinct transports, which implicitly differentiates the external path. But it doesn't explicitly call out what the agent should verify (e.g., transport-specific quirks, coercion edge cases).

steps.step: Find every site that depends on the tool-call contract

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensively enumerates every contract-dependent site with file:line: chat loop (chat_completion.rb:160-218, 290-299), function declaration (function_dispatch.rb:46-107), schema conversion (function_tool_adapter.rb:6-57), conversation adapters (message_adapters/base.rb, transcript_adapter.rb, multimodal), MCP external path (mcp.rb, mcp/tool.rb, sse_client.rb, stdio_client.rb), and a thorough spec matrix. Even adds PromptDeclarations and Predicate wrappers — the agent could change the contract safely from this list.

specificity.rationale: Pieces are named with concrete file:line ranges (e.g. 'chat_completion.rb:180-187', 'function_dispatch.rb:126-130', 'mcp.rb:108-110') and specific method names like `dispatch_tool_function`, `filtered_tools`, `convert_tools_for_ruby_llm` — not vague 'the tool code'.

justification.rationale: Section headings imply the role of each group (dispatch vs declaration vs schema vs adaptation vs external vs tests), and short parentheticals explain some entries (e.g. 'wraps functions as { type: function }', 'handles arrays from function dispatch'). However, per-site WHY is uneven — many lines are bare file:line without explaining their role in the contract.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flags about what static reading can miss — mixin composition into a host class, MCP transport variability, fixture coverage gaps. The list is presented with full confidence, no caveats inviting the agent to verify.

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive edit map covering all required areas (chat loop, function DSL+schema conversion, conversation adapters, external MCP path) plus specs, with file:line throughout. The cross-cutting concerns section (transcript shape and function definition hash sites) is exceptionally actionable; a teammate could open the PR from this alone.

specificity.rationale: Every entry names a concrete file with line ranges (e.g., 'lib/raix/chat_completion.rb:160-218', 'lib/raix/mcp.rb:131-152', 'spec/raix/mcp_spec.rb:356-396'). Tables are organized by area with precise change points rather than vague pointers.

justification.rationale: Each row includes a 'what to change' column tying the file to its role (e.g., 'Update if tools schema shape changes', 'detection + dispatch + continuation'). The cross-cutting sections explicitly explain why multiple sites must move together (transcript shape, function hash).

uncertainty.rationale: Flags review-only wrappers ('changes propagate automatically unless the method signature changes') and marks files like multimodal_content_adapter.rb and stdio_client.rb as 'No change expected unless...'. Could go further on schema/transport ambiguities, but conditional language is present throughout.
